top_findings.summary: The skill description for a "Vote Tracker" requests access to Bash (arbitrary shell command execution) and WebFetch (arbitrary network requests). These capabilities are excessive for a tool whose stated purpose is to record votes, track conditions, and manage follow-up items within an Investment Committee process. Such a tracking tool should only require Read/Write access to its own data files, not arbitrary system-level commands or external network access, which could be used to exfiltrate sensitive corporate data or perform unauthorized system operations.
